risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "On the basis of a computer-generated randomization sequence, patients recruited were assigned to 2 treatment groups." Comment: This explicitly states a computer-generated randomization sequence, which is a truly random method and is sufficient for Low risk of bias in random sequence generation.

Quote: "Trial authors confirm per email: allocation list was managed by an independent subject of the institution not directly involved in the trial (nurse)." Comment: This explicit description indicates that the allocation list was handled by an independent person not involved in enrolling participants, which would prevent recruiters from foreseeing upcoming assignments. The article itself is silent on concealment, but the author correspondence provides a clear concealment mechanism.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "placebo-controlled trial"; "Both DHEA and placebo were administrated throughout the whole period of ovarian stimulation"; trial authors confirm per email: "Study personnel and outcome assessors were blinded." Comment: This was a placebo-controlled randomized trial, and the authors/confirmatory correspondence indicate that personnel were blinded. Given the use of placebo and the fact that the main outcomes include clinical pregnancy/live birth plus IVF laboratory parameters, knowledge of allocation is unlikely to have influenced the dominant study outcomes overall.

Quote: "Trial authors confirm per email: allocation list was managed by an independent subject of the institution not directly involved in the trial (nurse). Study personnel and outcome assessors were blinded." Comment: The study explicitly states that outcome assessors were blinded, and the main outcomes were either objective record-based outcomes (live birth, miscarriage, clinical pregnancy, serum β-hCG) or standardized IVF measurements (e.g. oestradiol, endometrial thickness, oocyte counts). Knowledge of allocation is therefore unlikely to have influenced outcome measurement.

Quote: "All patients in both groups completed the study." Comment: The trial reports complete follow-up with no dropouts, withdrawals, or exclusions after randomisation, so there is no evidence of attrition-related bias.
